name: Namuyi

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Namuyi (namu1246).

notes: Skeleton. Namuyi is a Qiangic (Sino-Tibetan) language of Sichuan, China, written here in a Latin-based transcription. Base Latin consonants and vowels plus the digraphs ch/sh/ts/ng/ny and aspirates kh/ph/th are mapped. Lexical tone is not recovered by the romanization and is left unencoded, so an elevated PER floor is expected. Fine allophonic and vowel-quality detail of narrow transcription is only approximately covered.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Namuyi (namu1246)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
sources.notes: Classification reference for a skeleton-tier entry.
